text:
Call it vanity if you'd like, don't care.

Fact is this: you will only experience a limited number of youthful summers. Less than you think.

Get in phenomenal shape. Eat clean, perfect your movement, see what your body is capable of.

Don't waste another summer.

Ascend now.

High-agency people seem to have this weird immunity to embarrassment.

Getting rejected? Not embarrassing, that’s just data collection.

Looking naive? Not embarrassing, that’s just information asymmetry you’re fixing.

Breaking minor social rules? Not embarrassing, most rules

The main thing I miss on Cloudflare is a single [ Block IP ] button

It boggles my mind it's not there, you can see a traffic spike and a person doing 50,000 requests per minute, but you have to write a security rule and go through 10 steps to block it

Too difficult

Just let me

People are now sharing skills, not code.

The assumption is that code is cheap and personalized, while skills are the perfect abstraction for instructing agents on how to build code.

These guys are talking this literally:

Take a link to a skill and paste it here. It will

I'm working with Linkup. They are doing something fundamentally different:

They built their own web index from scratch, designed specifically for how AI consumes information.

A traditional search engine indexes full web pages. Your agent has to parse through all of the garbage

Nothing is more important than context.

Nothing beats good context if you want consistent, reliable results from agents.

If you give your agent access to the Internet, you make it 100x more powerful.

I think this is a pretty good idea:

Give people the ability to stitch together multiple steps into a workflow that's 10x more powerful than any individual prompt.

We already have super-powerful models that can do a lot of cool things, but we need better tools to harness them.

I write a short Saturday essay each week to help people build better lives.

One story or lesson on rethinking how you work, earn, and live.
